name: Gemini 3.5 Pro
tagline: Google's announced flagship Gemini 3.5 tier — previewed at I/O 2026, not yet shipped.
seoTitle: Gemini 3.5 Pro: Google's Flagship Gemini Model
metaDescription: Gemini 3.5 Pro is Google's announced flagship model from the Gemini 3.5 family, unveiled at Google I/O 2026. It is in internal preview — here is what Google ha…
license: Proprietary (Google) — access via Google API/products; no published terms for the Pro tier yet
overview: Gemini 3.5 Pro is the flagship tier Google announced for its Gemini 3.5 model family at Google I/O 2026 on May 19, 2026. At launch, Google shipped only the smaller sibling, Gemini 3.5 Flash, which is generally available. For Pro, Google's official announcement said the model was "already being used internally" and that the company looked forward to "rolling it out next month" — i.e., a planned June 2026 rollout.

As of June 2026, Gemini 3.5 Pro has not appeared in Google's public model documentation, the Gemini API model list, or the official pricing page. It remains in internal use and limited enterprise preview. Google has not published concrete specifications for the Pro tier: there is no officially confirmed context-window size, output limit, knowledge cutoff, parameter count, or benchmark figure for Gemini 3.5 Pro. Numbers circulating in third-party blogs are speculation and are deliberately omitted here.

What Google did say publicly positions Gemini 3.5 Pro as the family's frontier-intelligence model, taking over the high-end reasoning and multimodal use cases above Gemini 3.5 Flash. This page will be updated with verified context window, pricing, modalities, and benchmarks once Google publishes them at general availability.
strengths: Positioned by Google as the frontier-intelligence flagship of the Gemini 3.5 family, above the already-shipped Gemini 3.5 Flash
Backed by Google DeepMind and the Gemini 3.x lineage, which already ships strong multimodal and long-context models
Officially confirmed by Google as in active internal use ahead of a planned public rollout
useCases: Frontier reasoning and complex problem-solving tasks (the role Google positions Pro for, once available)
Multimodal understanding across text and images, per Google's stated direction for the model
Workloads that need the top Gemini tier rather than the faster, cheaper Gemini 3.5 Flash

status: Announced — internal preview, not yet generally available (as of June 2026)

faq.a: Not as a general release. Google announced it at I/O on May 19, 2026 and said it was being used internally with a rollout planned for the following month. As of June 2026 it had not appeared in Google's public model list, API, or pricing page — only Gemini 3.5 Flash shipped at announcement.

faq.q: What is the context window and price of Gemini 3.5 Pro?
faq.a: Google has not published either figure for the Pro tier. Any context-window size (such as a rumored 2M tokens) or per-token price you see is third-party speculation, not an official Google spec, so it is omitted here. The page will be updated when Google publishes verified numbers.

faq.q: How is Gemini 3.5 Pro different from Gemini 3.5 Flash?
faq.a: Gemini 3.5 Flash is the smaller, faster, cheaper model that Google released at I/O 2026 and made generally available. Gemini 3.5 Pro is the announced flagship tier positioned for frontier intelligence and the harder reasoning and multimodal tasks above Flash.

faq.q: What did Google officially say about Gemini 3.5 Pro?
faq.a: In its I/O 2026 announcements, Google wrote: "We're also hard at work on Gemini 3.5 Pro. It's already being used internally and we look forward to rolling it out next month." Google disclosed no specs, benchmarks, or pricing for the model at that time.
